items.title: US Dollar Index holds gains near 100.00 as traders focus on US jobs data and Iran conflict
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 100.00 during the early European trading hours on Friday. The DXY holds positive ground amid fresh concerns over a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: NZD/USD declines to near 0.5700 on weak Chinese PMI, US NFP data looms
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair extends the decline to a near four-month low around 0.5710 during the Asian trading hours on Friday. The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) softens against the US Dollar (USD) on the downbeat Chinese economic data and heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: WTI trades near $104.00 after 10% surge on Trump's Iran threats
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price rises over 10% after two days of losses, trading around $103.80 per barrel during the Asian hours on Friday. Crude oil prices surged as markets reassessed the scale of supply risks stemming from the ongoing conflict in the Persian Gulf.

items.title: Japanese Yen flat lines amid persistent tensions in the Middle East
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair trades on a flat note near 159.60 during the Asian trading hours on Friday. Trading volumes are likely to be thin due to the Good Friday holiday. All eyes will be on the US March Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report, which will be published later on Friday.

items.title: Australian Dollar moves little following China’s Services PMI
items.summary: AUD/USD gains ground after registering modest losses in the previous day, trading around 0.6910 during the Asian hours on Friday. The pair gains as the US Dollar (USD) softens, even amid stronger safe-haven demand due to escalating Middle East tensions.

items.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Oil price rise is quite serious, everything depends on how long it lasts
items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said on Thursday that he is concerned about the timing of rising oil prices, stating that they could hinder efforts to reduce inflation.

items.title: US President Donald Trump urges Iran to make deal after bridge strike
items.summary: US President Donald Trump touted the destruction of a bridge in Tehran, Iran. He warned that there was “much more to follow” and urged Tehran to “make a deal before it is too late."

items.title: US President Donald Trump will impose 100% tariff on some patented drugs — Bloomberg
items.summary: US President Donald Trump signed an executive order that could slap up to 100% on certain imported medicines from companies that don't reach deals with his administration in the coming months, Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

items.title: Indonesia: Surplus seen narrowing on external risks – UOB
items.summary: UOB’s Global Economics & Markets Research, led by Enrico Tanuwidjaja and Vincentius Ming Shen, notes that Indonesia’s trade surplus widened slightly in February 2026 to USD1.27bn, extending 70 months of gains.

items.title: South Korea: Inflation pressures seen building – ING
items.summary: ING’s Min Joo Kang notes that South Korea’s March consumer price inflation rose modestly, with government fuel caps and food vouchers limiting the impact of higher Oil costs. Core inflation eased slightly, but ING expects recent energy and currency moves to push prices higher in coming months.

items.title: NZD/USD weakens as Iran war tensions rise
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is trading around the 0.5710 region, maintaining a bearish tone amid heightened geopolitical tensions and risk aversion, as the US Dollar (USD) strengthens.

items.title: China: Export strength and bank flows – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur highlights that China’s economy started 2026 slightly better than expected, driven by a sharp rise in exports and a swelling current account surplus.
